reasoning: The Federal Reserve ended its balance sheet runoff in December 2025 and shifted to a policy of maintaining ample reserves by actively purchasing assets and reinvesting all maturing Treasuries and MBS principal into Treasury bills. Since December 2025, the total value of Treasury securities held by the Fed has increased for 13 consecutive weeks without exception, recently growing at an aggressive pace of $8B to $16B per week. Furthermore, daily TIPS inflation accruals ensure that even inactive weeks see slight upward adjustments. Given the active weekly buying program and the complete absence of runoff risk (due to full rollover policies at auction), the expected value for the week ending March 18 is virtually guaranteed to be significantly higher than the March 11 figure, barring an unprecedented settlement failure or sudden policy reversal within a single week.
